content.claim: The December 2023 anti-segregation law does not address free parental school choice under the voucher-financing model (Act Nkt.) or the selective six- and eight-grade gimnázium tracks, which critics argue remain the structural channels through which segregation strategies operate independent of SEN identification policy.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: The critical assessment is from opposition-aligned sources, not government self-assessment
The law's silence on school choice and elite tracks is a factual description of scope, but consequences for segregation are contested framing rather than measured outcome
No quantified impact assessment of the law on actual segregation patterns has been conducted yet
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
